title: Directives
content:
### What are Directives? Directives are classes that add behavior to elements in your Angular templates. There are three kinds: 1. **Components** ‚Äî Directives with a template (the most common). 2. **Structural directives** ‚Äî Change the DOM layout by adding/removing elements (`*ngIf`, `*ngFor`, `*ngSwitch`). 3. **Attribute directives** ‚Äî Change the appearance or behavior of an existing element (`ngClass`, `ngStyle`, `ngModel`). ### Structural Directives They begin with an asterisk (`*`) which is syntactic sugar for ``. **`*ngIf`** ‚Äî Conditionally renders an element: ```html
Welcome back!

Please log in.

 ``` **`*ngFor`** ‚Äî Iterates over a collection: ```html
  • {{ i }}: {{ item.name }}
``` **`*ngSwitch`** ‚Äî Similar to JavaScript switch: ```html

Admin Panel

User Dashboard

Guest View

``` ### Attribute Directives **`ngClass`** ‚Äî Dynamically add/remove CSS classes: ```html
Content
``` **`ngStyle`** ‚Äî Inline styles: ```html
Styled
``` **`ngModel`** ‚Äî Two-way data binding (requires `FormsModule`): ```html ``` ### Custom Directive ```typescript import { Directive, ElementRef, HostListener, HostBinding } from '@angular/core'; @Directive({ selector: '[appHighlight]', standalone: true }) export class HighlightDirective { @HostBinding('style.backgroundColor') bgColor = 'transparent'; @HostListener('mouseenter') onMouseEnter() { this.bgColor = 'yellow'; } @HostListener('mouseleave') onMouseLeave() { this.bgColor = 'transparent'; } } ``` Usage: `

Hover to highlight

` - **`@HostListener`** ‚Äî Listens to events on the host element. - **`@HostBinding`** ‚Äî Binds a class/property/attribute on the host element.

quiz.question: Which directive is used to conditionally render an element in Angular?
What decorator listens to events on the host element of a custom directive?
